prevalence.source: ESC Atlas of Cardiovascular Disease Statistics 2021; extrapolated to 2024 via WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: stable with slight decline due to risk factor control

incidence.source: ESC Guidelines for Chronic Coronary Syndromes 2019; ECDC surveillance data

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME); WHO Europe cardiovascular report

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on ESC 2019 Guidelines: Clinical symptoms (angina), ECG changes, stress testing, coronary angiography confirming >50% stenosis in major coronary arteries

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: 65% for high-risk groups (varies by member state)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 55% achieving LDL targets per ESC guidelines

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Harmonized per ESC 2019 Guidelines across EU, with national adaptations
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ESC-recommended: Optimal medical therapy (statins, antiplatelets), PCI/CABG for high-risk cases; dual antiplatelet therapy post-ACS
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Disparities in access between Western and Eastern EU; aging population increasing burden
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Advanced interventional cardiology networks; high adherence to ESC guidelines in most member states
